text: The most important distinction in the Hooked model is the shift from external to internal triggers. An external trigger is a push notification, an email badge, a red dot — it arrives from outside and commands attention. But product designers consider external triggers a crutch. The real goal is colonizing the user's internal emotional landscape: to become the default response to discomfort. When someone reaches for their phone during a moment of boredom without any external prompt, the product has graduated to internal trigger status. It no longer needs to interrupt — it has become the interruption users inflict on themselves.
question: According to the passage, what distinguishes an internal trigger from an external one in the Hooked model?

options.text: Internal triggers require no emotional investment from the user
Internal triggers are initiated by the platform rather than the user
Internal triggers are consciously chosen while external ones are not
Internal triggers arise from within the user with no external prompt required
